readings.title: The Managed Child
readings.argument: The founding territory: how the twentieth century turned childhood into something measured, conditioned, and administered — built almost entirely by the ordinary secular mainstream.

readings.title: The Aeon Current
readings.argument: Crowley outward: one received book, four idioms of an initiated elect, and the documented chain of hands it passed through in America — rocketry, a new religion, film, and the record of what was claimed about all of it.

readings.title: The Smoothing
readings.argument: The tidying reflex: files burned, testimony sanitized, retractions entered under law — and what the over-cleaning of a rough record makes people believe.

readings.title: The Recurring Doctrine
readings.argument: The elect-guiding-evolution claim in its most cosmic idiom, carried by one institution — the Theosophical Society — that fractured three ways in fifty years, and touched the machinery of childhood once, cleanly, through Montessori at Adyar.
